question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 56/6/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 33 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 33 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. Section A - questions number 1 to 16 are multiple choice type questions. Each question carries 1 mark.
  4. Section B - questions number 17 to 21 are very short answer type questions. Each question carries 2 marks.
  5. Section C - questions number 22 to 28 are short answer type questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
  6. Section D - questions number 29 and 30 are case-based questions. Each question carries 4 marks.
  7. Section E - questions number 31 to 33 are long answer type questions. Each question carries 5 marks.
  8. There is no overall choice given in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in few questions in all the sections except Section -A.
  9. Kindly note that there is a separate question paper for Visually Impaired candidates.
  10. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

Section - A

Questions no. 1 to 16 are Multiple Choice type Questions, carrying 1 mark each. (16 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 16)
1.

During dehydration of alcohol to alkene by heating with conc. {tex}{H}_{2} {SO}_{4}{/tex},  the initiation step is:

a. Formation of an ester
b. Formation of carbocation
c. Protonation of alcohol
d. Elimination of water
1
2.

Which reagents are required for one step conversion of Chlorobenzene to Diphenyl?

a. Chlorobenzene, Na, Dry ether
b. Benzene, Anhydrous {tex}{AlCl}_{3}{/tex}
c. Chlorobenzene/Fe, Dark
d. {tex}{NaNO}_{2}+{HCl}{/tex}
1
3.

Which of the following complexes show linkage isomerism?

a. {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5} {Cl}\right]^{2+}{/tex}
b. {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{4} {Cl}_{2}\right] {Br}_{2}{/tex}
c. {tex}\left[{Co}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}{/tex}
d. {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5}({ONO})\right]^{2+}{/tex}
1
4.

Which of the following transition metals has the highest melting point?

a. Sc
b. Cr
c. Mn
d. Zn
1
5.

The freezing point of one molal KCl solution, assuming KCl to be completely dissociated in water, is: {tex}\left({K}_{{f}}\right.{/tex} for water {tex}\left.=1.86 {~K} {~kg} {~mol}^{-1}\right){/tex}

a. {tex}-3 \cdot 72^{\circ} {C}{/tex}
b. {tex}+3 \cdot 72^{\circ} {C}{/tex}
c. {tex}-1.86^{\circ} {C}{/tex}
d. {tex}+2 \cdot 72^{\circ} {C}{/tex}
1
6.

The reagent that can be used to convert benzenediazonium chloride to benzene is:

a. {tex}{Cu} / {HCl}{/tex}
b. {tex}{H}_{2} {O}{/tex}
c. {tex}{CH}_{3} {CH}_{2} {OH}{/tex}
d. CuCN
1
7.

The strongest base in aqueous solution among the following amines is:

a. {tex}\left({C}_{2} {H}_{5}\right)_{2} {NH}{/tex}
b. {tex}\left({C}_{2} {H}_{5}\right)_{3} {~N}{/tex}
c. {tex}{C}_{2} {H}_{5} {NH}_{2}{/tex}
d. {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{5} {NH}_{2}{/tex}
1
8.

On mixing 30 mL of acetone with 20 mL of chloroform, the total volume of solution is:

a. equal to 10 mL
b. less than 50 mL
c. greater than 50 mL
d. equal to 50 mL
1
9.

We cannot measure the resistance of an ionic solution using DC because:

a. it changes the composition of the solution.
b. it can cause sparks and shocks.
c. it does not affect the composition of the solution.
d. it converts electrolytic cell to galvanic cell.
1
10.

The primary and secondary valences of Co in {tex}\left[{Co}({en})_{3}\right] {Cl}_{3}{/tex} respectively are:

a. 3, 3
b. 0, 3
c. 6, 3
d. 3, 6
1
11.

In the Arrhenius equation {tex}{k}={Ae}^{-{E}_{{a}} / {RT}}{/tex}, 'A' represents:

a. effective collisions
b. frequency factor
c. fraction of collisions
d. threshold energy
1
12.

{tex}\alpha{/tex}-D-glucose and {tex}\beta{/tex}-D-glucose differ from each other with respect to the:

a. size of the hemiacetal ring
b. configuration at the {tex}{C}_{2}{/tex} carbon
c. number of -OH groups
d. configuration at the {tex}{C}_{1}{/tex} carbon
1

For Questions number 13 to 16, two statements are given - one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R). Select the correct answer to these questions from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
13.

Assertion (A): Actinoid contraction is greater from element to element than lanthanoid contraction.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: Actinoids show wide range of oxidation states.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
14.

Assertion (A): Molecularity of reaction is determined experimentally.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: Molecularity is applicable only for an elementary reaction and not for a complex reaction.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
15.

Assertion (A): Boiling point of {tex}\left({C}_{2} {H}_{5}\right)_{2} {NH}{/tex} is lower than that of {tex}n-{C}_{4} {H}_{9} {NH}_{2}{/tex}.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: Hydrogen bonding is much more extensive in {tex}{n}-{C}_{4} {H}_{9} {NH}_{2}{/tex} as compared to {tex}\left({C}_{2} {H}_{5}\right)_{2} {NH}{/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
16.

Assertion (A): The bond angle {tex}{C}-{O}-{C}{/tex} in ethers is slightly greater than tetrahedral angle.
Reason {tex}(R){/tex}: This is because of the repulsive interaction between the two bulky alkyl groups.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

Section - B
17.

Define osmotic pressure. Why is measurement of osmotic pressure method preferred for the determination of molar masses of macromolecules such as proteins and polymers?

 2
18.

How do you explain the following?

  1. Presence of an aldehydic group in glucose.
  2. Presence of five -OH groups in glucose.
 2
19.

Draw the structures of product(s) in each of the following reactions:

 2
20.

Observe the graph in the given figure and answer the following questions:

  1. Predict the order of reaction.
  2. What is the slope of the curve?
 2
21.(i)

Write IUPAC names of the following coordination compounds:

  1. {tex}\left[{CoCl}_{2}({en})_{2}\right] {SO}_{4}{/tex}
  2. {tex}{K}_{3}\left[{Fe}\left({C}_{2} {O}_{4}\right)_{3}\right]{/tex}
 2

OR
(ii)

Differentiate between:

  1. Double salt and Complex compound
  2. Didentate ligand and Ambidentate ligand
 2

Section - C
22.

How can you obtain the following?

  1. Aniline from ammonium benzoate
  2. Benzene diazonium chloride from nitrobenzene
  3. 2,4,6-tribromoaniline from aniline
 3
23.

Give reasons for the following:

  1. The pH of aqueous NaCl increases when it is electrolysed.
  2. Unlike dry cell, mercury cell has a constant cell potential through its lifetime.
  3. Conductivity of solution decreases with dilution.
 3
24.

The rate of a reaction:
{tex}{A}+{B} \longrightarrow{/tex} product
is given below as a function of different initial concentrations of {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex}.

Experiment {tex}[{A}] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} {tex}[{~B}] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} Initial Rate/mol L-1 min-1
1 0.01 0.01 5{tex}\times{/tex}10-3
2 0.02 0.01 1{tex}\times{/tex}10-2
3 0.01 0.02 5{tex}\times{/tex}10-3

Calculate the order of the reaction with respect to A and B. Determine the rate constant of the reaction.

 3
25.

Write any two differences between {tex}S_{N} 1{/tex} and {tex}S_{N} 2{/tex} reactions. Which of the following compounds would undergo {tex}{S}_{{N}} 1{/tex} reaction faster and why?

 3
26.

A compound (A) with molecular formula {tex}{C}_{4} {H}_{5} {~N}{/tex} on reduction with DIBAL-H followed by hydrolysis, gives a compound (B). Compound (B) gives positive Tollens' test but does not give iodoform test. Compound (B) can also be obtained when ethanal is treated with dilute NaOH followed by heating. Identify (A) and (B). Write the reactions of (A) with DIBAL-H followed by hydrolysis.

 3
27.(i)

Answer the following about the complexes
{tex}\left[\mathrm{FeF}_6\right]^{3-} \text { and }\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{4-}:{/tex}

  1. Write the hybridization involved in each case.
  2. Which of them is the outer orbital complex and which one is the inner orbital complex?
  3. Compare their magnetic behaviour.
    [Atomic number: {tex}{Fe}=26{/tex}]
 3

OR
(ii)
  1. What happens to the colour of complex {tex}\left[{Ti}\left({H}_{2} {O}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}{/tex} when heated gradually?
  2. Write the electronic configuration for {tex}{d}^{5}{/tex} ion if {tex}\Delta_{0} \lt {P}{/tex}.
  3. Write the hybridization and magnetic behaviour of the complex {tex}\left[{Ni}({CO})_{4}\right]{/tex}.
    [Atomic number: {tex}{Ni}=28{/tex}]
 3
28.

Vapour pressure of pure water at 298 K is 24.8 mm Hg. Calculate the lowering in vapour pressure of an aqueous solution which freezes at {tex}-0 \cdot 3^{\circ} {C} .\left({K}_{{f}}\right.{/tex} of water {tex}\left.=1 \cdot 86 {~K} {~kg} {~mol}^{-1}\right){/tex}

 3

Section - D

The following questions are case-based questions. Read the case carefully and answer the questions that follow.
29.

Alcohols undergo a number of reactions involving the cleavage of {tex}{C}-{OH}{/tex} bond. However, phenols do not undergo reactions involving the cleavage of {tex}{C}-{OH}{/tex} bond. Alcohols are weaker acids than water. Alcohols react with halogen acids to form the corresponding haloalkanes. Phenols are stronger acids than alcohols. A characteristic feature of phenols is that they undergo electrophilic substitution reactions such as halogenation, nitration, etc. Since - OH group is a strong activating group, phenol gives trisubstituted products during halogenation, nitration, etc.

  1. What happens when phenol is treated with the following? (2)
    1. {tex}{Br}_{2}{/tex} water
    2. Conc. {tex}{HNO}_{3}{/tex}
    1. Write the mechanism of alcohol reacting as nucleophile in a reaction with {tex}{CH}_{3}^{\oplus}{/tex}. (1)

      OR

    2. Why do phenols not undergo reactions involving cleavage of {tex}{C}-{OH}{/tex} bond? (1)
  3. How can you distinguish between Butan-1-ol and 2-Methylpropan-2-ol by using HCl in the presence of anhydrous {tex}{ZnCl}_{2}{/tex}? (1)
 5
30.

The {tex}\alpha{/tex}-amino acids are the building blocks of proteins. All {tex}\alpha{/tex}-amino acids exist as zwitter ion due to which they show amphoteric behaviour. All amino acids are joined through peptide bond. Proteins are broadly classified as globular proteins and fibrous proteins. Globular proteins are water soluble, whereas fibrous proteins are not. The complete structure of protein is discussed at four different levels i.e. primary, secondary, tertiary and quaternary structures. Protein loses its biological activity in denatured form.

  1. Define the following: (2)
    1. Peptide linkage
    2. Denatured protein
  2. Why do amino acids show amphoteric behaviour? (1)
    1. How can you differentiate between Fibrous protein and Globular protein? (1)

      OR

    2. Write the names of two different secondary structures of proteins. (1)
 5

Section - E
31.(i)
  1. Account for the following:
    1. Orange colour of {tex}{Cr}_{2} {O}_{7}{ }^{2-}{/tex} ion changes to yellow when treated with an alkali.
    2. {tex}{Zn}, {Cd}{/tex} and Hg are non-transition elements.
    3. {tex}{E}^{\circ}{/tex} value for {tex}{Mn}^{3+} / {Mn}^{2+}{/tex} couple is highly positive {tex}(+1 \cdot 57 {~V}){/tex} as compared to {tex}{Cr}^{3+} / {Cr}^{2+}{/tex}.
  2. What happens when:
    1. Manganate ion undergoes disproportionation reaction in acidic medium?
    2. {tex}{KMnO}_{4}{/tex} is heated?
 5

OR
31.(ii)

Answer the following questions:

  1. What is 'Misch metal'? Give its one use.
  2. Write the formula of an oxoanion of chromium in which it shows the oxidation state equal to its group number.
  3. Why does Vanadium pentoxide {tex}\left({V}_{2} {O}_{5}\right){/tex} act as a catalyst?
  4. Why do transition elements have high enthalpies of atomisation?
  5. How do you prepare {tex}{Na}_{2} {Cr}_{2} {O}_{7}{/tex} from {tex}{Na}_{2} {CrO}_{4}{/tex}?
 5
32.(i)
  1. Identify {tex}{A}, {B}{/tex} and C in the following reactions:
  2. Give reasons for the following:
    1. Carboxylic acids do not give the characteristic reactions of carbonyl group.
    2. Ethanoic acid is a stronger acid than ethanol.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Write the product(s) in the following reactions:
    1. {tex}2 {CH}_{3} {COOH} \xrightarrow[\text { heat }]{{P}_{4} {O}_{10}}{/tex}
  2. Write the reaction involved in the following reactions:
    1. Wolff-Kishner Reduction
    2. Decarboxylation Reaction
 5
33.(i)
  1. Calculate {tex}{E}_{\text {cell }}{/tex} of a galvanic cell in which the following reaction takes place at {tex}25^{\circ} {C}{/tex}:
    {tex}{Zn}({s})+{Pb}^{2+}(0 \cdot 02 {M}) \longrightarrow {Zn}^{2+}(0 \cdot 1 {M})+{Pb}({s}){/tex}
    ［Given: {tex}{E}_{{Zn}^{2+} / {Zn}}^{\circ}=-0 \cdot 76 {~V}, {E}_{{Pb}^{2+} / {Pb}}^{\circ}=-0 \cdot 13 {~V}{/tex};
    {tex}\log 2=0 \cdot 3010, \log 4=0 \cdot 6021, \log 5=0 \cdot 6990]{/tex}.
  2. State Faraday's first law of electrolysis. How much electricity, in terms of Faraday, is required to reduce one mol of {tex}{MnO}_{4}^{-}{/tex}to {tex}{Mn}^{2+}{/tex} ion?
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. The resistance of a conductivity cell containing 0.001 M KCl solution at 298 K is 1000 ohm. What is the cell constant if conductivity of 0.001 M KCl solution at 298 K is {tex}0.125 \times 10^{-3} {~S} {~cm}^{-1}{/tex}?
  2. Calculate the {tex}{E}_{{Mg}^{2+} / {Mg}}{/tex} potential for the following half cell at {tex}25^{\circ} {C}{/tex}:
    {tex} {Mg} / {Mg}^{2+}\left(1 \times 10^{-4} {M}\right) ; {E}_{{Mg}^{2+} / {Mg}}^{\circ}=+2.36 {~V} {/tex}
    [Given : {tex}\log 10=1{/tex}]
  3. What is the effect of temperature on the electrical conductance of metallic conductor?
 5
